commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Chanaan. These unnatural sacrifices were perhaps practised under the judges, or at least under the kings. (Worthington)
Some pretend that the children were only made to pass over or between fires. But this is refuted by facts, Leviticus xviii. 21., and 4 Kings xvi. 3., and Jeremias vii. 30
Polluted. Infecta: ephonoktanethe is used by Septuagint, in this sense, though it signifies also, "was slain "interfecta, as most Latin Bibles and Fathers read, before the correction of Clement VIII. The former expression is more natural and conformable to the Hebrew. (Calmet)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
